message:
perf(sync): add scan_start cursor to Notify::notify_one for O(1) amortized wakeup

WaiterSlab now tracks a scan_start hint — the lower bound of where the
first active (non-notified, has-waker) entry might be. notify_one() starts
scanning from this position instead of index 0, advancing it past each
notification. New inserts at indices below the cursor reset it.

This makes sequential notify_one() calls O(1) amortized instead of O(n)
when many cancelled/notified waiters leave holes at the front of the slab.
All 238 sync tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
